react.answer:
## Building a Collapsible Sidebar Menu in React **Mental model:** One boolean — `collapsed` — drives the whole component. Collapsed shrinks the width and hides labels (icons remain); expanded shows both. Submenus track their own open set. CSS transitions on `width` make it feel smooth; labels are hidden, not unmounted, so the animation stays clean. ### Step 1 — Collapsed state + width ```jsx const [collapsed, setCollapsed] = useState(false);
``` ### Step 2 — Items with icon + (conditional) label ```jsx {item.icon}{!collapsed && {item.label}} ``` ### Step 3 — Expandable submenus Track open groups in a `Set`; only relevant when expanded. ```jsx const [openGroups, setOpenGroups] = useState(new Set()); ``` ### Step 4 — Toggle button ```jsx ``` **Edge cases** | Case | Handling | | :-- | :-- | | Submenu while collapsed | Hide submenus (or show as flyout) when collapsed. | | Active route | Highlight the current item. | | a11y | `aria-expanded` on the toggle and group headers. | **Interview tip:** Hiding labels via conditional render while keeping the icons mounted (and transitioning `width`) avoids layout jank — note you'd add `aria-expanded`/`title` so collapsed icons stay accessible.

vue.answer:
## Building a Collapsible Sidebar Menu in Vue 3 **Mental model:** A `collapsed` boolean drives the width and label visibility; icons always show. Submenus track an open `Set`. CSS transitions on `width` keep it smooth. Labels are `v-if`'d off when collapsed. ### Step 1 — Collapsed state + width ```vue
``` ### Step 2 — Icon + conditional label ```vue {{ item.icon }} {{ item.label }} ``` ### Step 3 — Expandable submenus ```js const openGroups = ref(new Set(['lib'])); function toggleGroup(id) { const n = new Set(openGroups.value); n.has(id) ? n.delete(id) : n.add(id); openGroups.value = n; } ``` ### Step 4 — Toggle ```vue ``` **Edge cases** | Case | Handling | | :-- | :-- | | Submenu collapsed | Hide submenus when collapsed. | | Active item | Highlight current. | | a11y | `aria-expanded` on toggles. | **Interview tip:** Transition `width` while `v-if`-ing labels (icons stay) for a jank-free collapse; mention `:title` on icons so the collapsed state remains discoverable.

files./src/App.vue:
<script setup>
import Sidebar from './components/Sidebar.vue';
</script>

<template>
  <Sidebar />
</template>

<style>
body {
  margin: 0;
}
</style>

angular.answer:
## Building a Collapsible Sidebar Menu in Angular **Mental model:** A `collapsed` boolean drives width + label visibility; icons always show. Submenus track an open `Set`. CSS transitions on `width` keep it smooth; labels use `*ngIf`. Classic `NgModule` setup, no `?.`/`??` (the build rejects them). ### Step 1 — Collapsed state + width ```html
``` ### Step 2 — Icon + conditional label ```html {{ item.icon }} {{ item.label }} ``` ### Step 3 — Expandable submenus ```typescript openGroups = new Set(['lib']); toggleGroup(id: string): void { if (this.openGroups.has(id)) { this.openGroups.delete(id); } else { this.openGroups.add(id); } } ``` ### Step 4 — Toggle ```html ``` **Edge cases** | Case | Handling | | :-- | :-- | | Submenu collapsed | Hide when collapsed. | | Active item | Highlight current. | | a11y | `[attr.aria-expanded]` on toggles. | **Interview tip:** Transition `width` while `*ngIf`-ing labels (icons stay) avoids layout jank; `[style.width.px]` is the clean Angular way to bind the animated width.
